verses.text: Thirteen years after Ishmael's birth, God appears to Abram, now 99. He introduces a new name for Himself: El Shaddai, God Almighty. Walk before me, and be perfect — the call deepens. Faith is not a one-time decision but a lifelong walk.

Thirteen years after Ishmael's birth, God appears to Abram, now 99. He introduces a new name for Himself: El Shaddai, God Almighty. Walk before me, and be perfect — the call deepens. Faith is not a one-time decision but a lifelong walk.

Abram falls on his face. And God says: your name will no longer be Abram, but Abraham — father of many nations. The promise gets sewn into his very identity. He cannot say his own name now without saying God's promise.

Abram falls on his face. And God says: your name will no longer be Abram, but Abraham — father of many nations. The promise gets sewn into his very identity. He cannot say his own name now without saying God's promise.

Abram falls on his face. And God says: your name will no longer be Abram, but Abraham — father of many nations. The promise gets sewn into his very identity. He cannot say his own name now without saying God's promise.

Nations will come from him. Kings from his line. The land of Canaan, an everlasting possession. And the deepest promise of all: I will be a God unto you and your offspring after you. The covenant is personal, not just material.

Nations will come from him. Kings from his line. The land of Canaan, an everlasting possession. And the deepest promise of all: I will be a God unto you and your offspring after you. The covenant is personal, not just material.

Nations will come from him. Kings from his line. The land of Canaan, an everlasting possession. And the deepest promise of all: I will be a God unto you and your offspring after you. The covenant is personal, not just material.

Now Abraham's part. Every male in his household must be circumcised — a sign cut into the very flesh of the body that produces the promised offspring. The covenant is not abstract. It is written on the body.

Now Abraham's part. Every male in his household must be circumcised — a sign cut into the very flesh of the body that produces the promised offspring. The covenant is not abstract. It is written on the body.

Now Abraham's part. Every male in his household must be circumcised — a sign cut into the very flesh of the body that produces the promised offspring. The covenant is not abstract. It is written on the body.
Eight days old for newborns. Bought servants too. Anyone who refuses the sign is cut off from the people. Belonging to this covenant has a permanent mark — visible to no one but oneself and God.
Eight days old for newborns. Bought servants too. Anyone who refuses the sign is cut off from the people. Belonging to this covenant has a permanent mark — visible to no one but oneself and God.
Eight days old for newborns. Bought servants too. Anyone who refuses the sign is cut off from the people. Belonging to this covenant has a permanent mark — visible to no one but oneself and God.
Sarai's name also changes — to Sarah. Princess. I will bless her, give her a son, make her a mother of nations. Notice: kings of people shall be of her. The promise is hers, not only Abraham's.
Sarai's name also changes — to Sarah. Princess. I will bless her, give her a son, make her a mother of nations. Notice: kings of people shall be of her. The promise is hers, not only Abraham's.
Abraham falls on his face — and laughs. A hundred years old, and Sarah ninety? He doesn't doubt God in speech, but his body and his math do the doubting for him. Faith and laughter mingle.
Then Abraham makes a quieter prayer: O that Ishmael might live before thee. He is already attached to the son he has. He doesn't yet realize how much bigger the answer will be.

No, says God, Sarah will bear you a son and you will name him Isaac — meaning he laughs. The covenant goes through him. As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him too, and twelve princes will come from him. But the promise runs through Isaac.

No, says God, Sarah will bear you a son and you will name him Isaac — meaning he laughs. The covenant goes through him. As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him too, and twelve princes will come from him. But the promise runs through Isaac.

No, says God, Sarah will bear you a son and you will name him Isaac — meaning he laughs. The covenant goes through him. As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him too, and twelve princes will come from him. But the promise runs through Isaac.

God goes up from Abraham. Same day, Abraham takes Ishmael and every man in his household and circumcises them all. There is no delay between command and obedience — the man who once compromised in Egypt does not flinch here.

God goes up from Abraham. Same day, Abraham takes Ishmael and every man in his household and circumcises them all. There is no delay between command and obedience — the man who once compromised in Egypt does not flinch here.
Abraham is 99, Ishmael is 13, and every male in the household is circumcised the same day. The chapter closes with quiet, complete obedience. The promise has been received and sealed in flesh.
